entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The relationship between block sizes, transaction fees, and mining rewards is complex and non-linear, as evidenced by various academic studies on the Nash equilibrium of these factors. It has been established that neither zero nor infinite block sizes are optimal. Instead, there exists a spectrum where certain block sizes maximize fee efficiency without collapsing the system. For instance, smaller block sizes can lead to higher transaction fees because of increased competition among transactions to be included in the next block. This dynamic was illustrated through an example where a block size that accommodates fewer transactions results in significantly higher fees for those transactions due to reduced block space availability.
Furthermore, the profitability of miners is directly affected by block sizes and associated fees. If transaction fees fall too low, such as 1 sat/byte, it may not be economically viable for miners to continue operations, leading to a decrease in hash rate. A lower hash rate undermines the security of the blockchain, highlighting the delicate balance required in setting block sizes. The discussion also touches upon technological interventions like mweb and other fee-driving technologies that could potentially enhance miner profitability and blockchain security without resorting to solutions like inflation, which is generally viewed unfavorably.
